findings.description: Repeated page header/footer content in HTML
findings.evidence: The following text blocks repeat 3+ times, likely page headers/footers that should not appear in the body: 'where,' (3x).

findings.description: Figure 3 flowchart content is rendered both as an image and as a duplicate text-based bullet-point rendition in the HTML, creating a reading-order duplication.
findings.evidence: PDF shows Figure 3 only as a graphical flowchart. HTML shows Figure 3 as an image AND as bullet-point text below ('Was a 24-hr composite sample collected? YES: Use 24-hr composite sample concentration* ... NO: Use 60-day rolling average dry concentration'), duplicating the same content.

findings.description: Figure 3 (Flowchart for Determining the Concentration for Loading at the River Water Intake) from PDF page 17 is missing as an image in the HTML. Instead, its content is rendered as a text-based list, but no tag is present for this figure.
findings.evidence:
PDF page 17 contains a full flowchart image for Figure 3. The HTML has tags for Figures 1 and 2 but replaces Figure 3 with: '
  • Was a 24-hr composite sample collected?
    • YES: Use 24-hr composite sample concentration*...
    • NO: Use 60-day rolling average dry concentration
'

findings.description: In Equations 2 and 3, the PDF distinguishes between uppercase C (total PFAS concentration) and lowercase c (individual compound concentration). The HTML renders both as uppercase C, losing this meaningful notational distinction.
findings.evidence: PDF Equation 2 second line: 'Σ c_OF002,j,i × Q_OF002,j × F' (lowercase c). HTML renders: 'Σ (from i=1 to I) C<sub>OF002,j,i</sub> × Q<sub>OF002,j</sub> × F' (uppercase C). Same issue in Equation 3 where PDF 'c_intake,k,i' becomes HTML 'C<sub>intake,k,i</sub>'. The document explicitly defines C as total concentration and c as individual compound concentration.
